items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.hook: Marry Me, Cherie is a 1964 German-Austrian comedy film directed by Axel von Ambesser and starring Paul Hubschmid, Letícia Román and Ann Smyrner.
items.body: It is based on a novel by Gábor Vaszary. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

items.hook: “He had a big impact on the history of film in this country”: Industry pays tribute to Simon Field who had died aged 80
items.body: “Simon never gave up, never compromised,” said Apichatpong Weerasethakul of his ‘Uncle Boomee’ producer.

items.hook: Moon Over Morocco is a 1931 French mystery film directed by Julien Duvivier and starring Harry Baur, René Lefèvre and Rosine Deréan.
items.body: A separate German-language film The Five Accursed Gentlemen was also produced. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Bad Bunny concert in Milan abandoned because of hailstorm
items.body:
Fans evacuated from sold-out open-air show as organisers promise full refunds

An open-air show by the Puerto Rican music star Bad Bunny was abandoned in Italy on Saturday night because of a violent hailstorm, but the organiser said

Suzaki Paradise: Red Light District or Suzaki Paradise: Akashingō is a 1956 Japanese drama film directed by Yūzō Kawashima. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Inugami Family is a 1976 Japanese mystery film directed by Kon Ichikawa.
items.body: It is based on the novel The Inugami Curse by Seishi Yokomizo. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Judge orders pause on Paramount-Warner merger after challenge from 12 states
items.body:
$81bn merger halted for at least two weeks after US states sued to block deal, saying it would ‘extinguish competition’

items.hook: Ninth Building is a novel by Zou Jingzhi.
items.body: Originally written in Mandarin Chinese, it was translated to English by Jeremy Tiang and published by Honford Star in 2023. One of those things you can't un-know.
